Interface Channel

All Superinterfaces:
GetPerlChannel
public non-sealed interface Channel extends GetPerlChannel
Channel is a combined API that exposes both producer and consumer behavior for the PerL internals. It extends GetPerlChannel to allow obtaining per-thread channels and adds methods used by internal recorder threads to receive timestamped events.

Semantics:

  • receive(int) blocks up to timeout milliseconds waiting for the next TimeStamp to be available. If no data is available within the timeout it may return null.
  • sendEndTime(long) is used to notify the channel consumers that the benchmark is ending; implementations should use this to unblock waiting consumers where appropriate.
  • clear() should remove any queued data and reset internal state so the channel can be reused.
Note: caller-side thread usage should follow the guideline that each recording thread has its own PerlChannel instance obtained via GetPerlChannel.getPerlChannel().

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clear()
    Clear the channel of any pending data and reset its internal state.
    TimeStamp
    receive(int timeout)
    Receive the benchmarking timestamp.
    void
    sendEndTime(long endTime)
    Send an end time indication to consumers.

  • Method Details

    • receive

      TimeStamp receive(int timeout)
      Receive the benchmarking timestamp.
      Parameters:
      timeout - maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the data.
      Returns:
      TimeStamp Time stamp data or null if none available within the timeout

    • sendEndTime

      void sendEndTime(long endTime)
      Send an end time indication to consumers. This is typically invoked by the producer to signal shutdown.
      Parameters:
      endTime - End Time.

    • clear

      void clear()
      Clear the channel of any pending data and reset its internal state.
